meaning: adj.有同情心的,合意的,赞成的n.[解]交感神经,容易感受的人
examples: Tourists are, in the main, sympathetic people.
I did not feel at all sympathetic towards Kate.
She was very sympathetic to the problems of adult students.
Despite what you may think, most companies are sympathetic to people who cannot afford repayments.
Mr Lissouba has visited Congo's President Laurent Kabila several times recently, and appeared to get a sympathetic reception.
